content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and KKK. Find any two integers aaa and bbb such that


1‚Č§a,b‚Č§N1 \leq a, b \leq N1‚Č§a,b‚Č§N,
‚ą£a‚ąíb‚ą£‚Č•K|a - b| \geq K‚ą£a‚ąíb‚ą£‚Č•K, and
‚ą£gcd‚Ā°(a,b)‚ąílcm(a,b)‚ą£‚Č•2K|\gcd(a, b) - \text{lcm}(a, b)| \geq 2K‚ą£gcd(a,b)‚ąílcm(a,b)‚ą£‚Č•2K
That is, your task is to find any two integers not exceeding NNN, such that they differ by at least KKK and their GCD and LCM differ by at least 2K2K2K.

It is possible that no valid (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) exist, in which case you must print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 twice instead.

As a reminder,


gcd‚Ā°(a,b)\gcd(a, b)gcd(a,b) denotes the greatest common divisor of aaa and bbb.
lcm(a,b)\text{lcm}(a, b)lcm(a,b) denotes the least common multiple of aaa and bbb.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, on a new line:



If no valid pair exists, print the integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 twice, separated by a space.
Otherwise, print the integers aaa and bbb you found, separated by a space. These two integers should satisfy all the conditions laid out in the statement.

If multiple valid pairs exist, you may find any of them.

Since K=2K = 2K=2 here, 2‚Č•K2 \geq K2‚Č•K and 10‚Č•2K10 \geq 2K10‚Č•2K, so this pair of elements is valid.
Test case 222: With a=10,b=3a = 10, b = 3a=10,b=3, we have ‚ą£a‚ąíb‚ą£=7‚Č•K|a - b| = 7 \geq K‚ą£a‚ąíb‚ą£=7‚Č•K and ‚ą£gcd‚Ā°(a,b)‚ąílcm(a,b)‚ą£=29‚Č•2K|\gcd(a, b) - \text{lcm}(a, b)| = 29 \geq 2K‚ą£gcd(a,b)‚ąílcm(a,b)‚ą£=29‚Č•2K.
